name: Tomato Hornworm Wipeouts
recipeInstructions:
1. With an adult's help, use a carrot peeler to remove the cucumber skin and create long thin strips along the entire length of the cucumber until you reach the seedy core. Set these strips aside.
2. Dice the seedy core into tiny pieces about the size of a pea.
3. Remove the top of the tomato by cutting it off, similar to making a jack-o-lantern on a pumpkin. Ask an adult to carefully cut 1 inch from the stem end with a paring knife, pointing the knife downward.
4. Scoop out the tomato's center using a spoon, leaving a large bowl-shaped space inside. Chop the insides into tiny pieces and set them aside.
5. Tear the string cheese into strips about the width of a pencil. Cut each strip into thirds and place them aside.
6. To make hornworms, spread thin layers of cream cheese on both sides of a cucumber strip using a butter knife. Wrap the cucumber in a coiling fashion around a piece of string cheese from one end to the other.
7. Blend any remaining cream cheese with 1-2 tablespoons of your favorite salad dressing and stir into the cucumber and tomato mixture. Spoon this into the hollowed-out tomato and refrigerate any extra.
8. Place the tomato at the center of a plate. Ask an adult to carefully cut three or four holes in various places on the tomato, matching the size of the worms.
9. Insert the worms into the holes and arrange them on the plate. For a sicko serving suggestion, cover the plate with torn lettuce leaves and arrange the worms crawling through the gnawed-out holes. Use cream cheese to seal one worm inside a folded leaf as if it had made a cocoon.
